---
title: "Species Distribution Modeling Results"
subtitle: "*Camponotus truncatus* (Spinola, 1808)"
author: "SDMtune MaxEnt Pipeline"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.align = 'center')
library(knitr)
library(rio)

# Set species information
species_key <- "1313954"
species_name <- "Camponotus truncatus (Spinola, 1808)"
species_folder <- paste0("../species/", species_key, "_Camponotus_truncatus_(Spinola,_1808)/SDM_maxnet")

# Load summary data
summary_data <- rio::import(file.path(species_folder, "tables", paste0("summary_", species_key, ".xlsx")))
```

# Overview

## Introduction

**Species:** *Camponotus truncatus* (Spinola, 1808)
**Common name:** Doorkeeper ant
**GBIF Key:** `r species_key`

**Modeling approach:**

 - Algorithm: MaxEnt (Maxnet implementation).
 
 - Framework: SDMtune R package.
 
 - Hyperparameter optimization: Genetic algorithm
 
 - Variable selection: Correlation-based (Spearman, threshold 0.7)
 
 - Cross-validation: 5-fold spatial splitting

---

## Study Objectives

1. Model current distribution based on climate and environmental variables
2. Project suitable habitat under future climate scenarios (SSP585 2021-2040)
3. Assess climate change impacts on species distribution
4. Identify key environmental predictors
5. Quantify model uncertainty and extrapolation risk

# Data Overview

## Occurrence Data - Geographic Distribution
```{r distribution, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("distribution_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Geographic distribution of occurrence records used for modeling. Points show cleaned and spatially thinned presence locations after quality control using CoordinateCleaner.

---

## Data Quality & Processing
```{r data-inspection, out.width='75%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("data_inspection_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Processing steps:**
- Downloaded from multiple biodiversity databases (GBIF, iNaturalist, BISON, ALA, iDigBio)
- Quality filters: removed invalid coordinates, sea points, duplicates, outliers
- Spatial thinning: one point per raster cell (removes spatial autocorrelation)
- Temporal filter: records from 1960 onwards

# Variable Selection

## Initial Variable Importance (Before Selection)
```{r initial-variables, out.width='70%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("initial_variable_importance_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Permutation importance of all environmental variables before correlation-based selection. Shows which variables contribute most to model predictions when using the full variable set.

---

## Variable Selection Process

**Method:** Correlation-based selection using Spearman's rank correlation

**Threshold:** Variables correlated >0.7 are candidates for removal

**Selection criterion:** Retain variables with highest permutation importance

**Purpose:**
- Reduce multicollinearity
- Prevent model overfitting
- Improve model interpretability
- Maintain predictive performance

---

## Final Selected Variables
```{r final-variables, out.width='70%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("final_variable_importance_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Final set of environmental predictors after correlation-based selection. These uncorrelated variables maximize model performance while minimizing redundancy.

# Model Performance

## Hyperparameter Optimization

**Optimization method:** Genetic algorithm (SDMtune)

**Hyperparameters tuned:**
- Feature classes: Linear (L), Quadratic (Q), Product (P), Hinge (H), and combinations
- Regularization multiplier: 0.5 to 10 (controls model complexity)

**Optimization metric:** AUC (Area Under the ROC Curve)

**Cross-validation:** 5-fold spatial splitting

---

## ROC Curve - Model Discrimination
```{r roc-curve, out.width='70%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("roc_curve_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Interpretation:**
- **AUC = 1.0:** Perfect discrimination
- **AUC = 0.5:** Random prediction (diagonal line)
- **Higher AUC:** Better ability to distinguish presence from background

## Jackknife Analysis - Variable Contributions
```{r jackknife, out.width='75%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("jackknife_plot_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Jackknife test showing variable contributions to model gain:
- **Dark bars:** Training gain with only that variable
- **Light bars:** Training gain without that variable
- **Red bar:** Training gain with all variables

Variables where the dark bar is long have the most information when used alone. Variables where the light bar is short are most important for the full model.

# Environmental Responses

## Response Curves - Overview

**Response curves** show how predicted suitability changes along environmental gradients while holding other variables at their average values.

**Two types:**
- **Marginal response:** Shows effect of varying only that variable
- **Response with all variables:** Shows effect while other variables covary

---
```{r response-bio4, out.width='80%', eval=file.exists(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO4_Temperature_Seasonality.png"))}
cat("## Climate Response - Temperature Seasonality\n\n")
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO4_Temperature_Seasonality.png"))
```

---
```{r response-bio5, out.width='80%', eval=file.exists(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO5_Max_Temperature_Warmest_Month.png"))}
cat("## Climate Response - Max Temperature Warmest Month\n\n")
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO5_Max_Temperature_Warmest_Month.png"))
```

---
```{r response-bio8, out.width='80%', eval=file.exists(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO8_Mean_Temperature_Wettest_Quarter.png"))}
cat("## Climate Response - Mean Temperature Wettest Quarter\n\n")
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO8_Mean_Temperature_Wettest_Quarter.png"))
```

---
```{r response-bio15, out.width='80%', eval=file.exists(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO15_Precipitation_Seasonality.png"))}
cat("## Precipitation Response - Seasonality\n\n")
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO15_Precipitation_Seasonality.png"))
```

---
```{r response-bio19, out.width='80%', eval=file.exists(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO19_Precipitation_Coldest_Quarter.png"))}
cat("## Precipitation Response - Coldest Quarter\n\n")
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_BIO19_Precipitation_Coldest_Quarter.png"))
```

---
```{r response-shrubs, out.width='80%', eval=file.exists(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_Shrubs.png"))}
cat("## Land Cover Response - Shrubs\n\n")
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_Shrubs.png"))
```

---
```{r response-deciduous, out.width='80%', eval=file.exists(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_Deciduous_Broadleaf_Trees.png"))}
cat("## Land Cover Response - Deciduous Broadleaf Trees\n\n")
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_Deciduous_Broadleaf_Trees.png"))
```

---
```{r response-cultivated, out.width='80%', eval=file.exists(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_Cultivated_Managed_Vegetation.png"))}
cat("## Land Cover Response - Cultivated Vegetation\n\n")
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, "response_curves", "response_Cultivated_Managed_Vegetation.png"))
```

# Current Distribution

## Current Habitat Suitability - Global (Clamped)
```{r current-global-clamped, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("current_clamped_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Predicted habitat suitability under current climate conditions (1970-2000). **Clamped predictions** constrain environmental values to the range observed in training data, providing conservative estimates. Values range from 0 (unsuitable) to 1 (highly suitable).

---

## Current Habitat Suitability - Europe Focus (Clamped)
```{r current-europe-clamped, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("current_europe_clamped_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Regional view focusing on Europe, showing detailed distribution patterns and habitat suitability gradients across the study region.

---

## Current Distribution - Unclamped Predictions
```{r current-global-unclamped, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("current_unclamped_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** **Unclamped predictions** allow model extrapolation beyond training data range. Useful for understanding model behavior but may produce unrealistic values in novel environments. Compare with clamped version to assess extrapolation effects.

# Model Uncertainty

## Prediction Uncertainty Across CV Folds
```{r uncertainty, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("uncertainty_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Standard deviation of predictions across 5 cross-validation folds. Higher values (warmer colors) indicate greater disagreement between model replicates, suggesting lower confidence in predictions. Low uncertainty areas are more reliable.

---

## MESS Analysis - Extrapolation Risk
```{r mess, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("mess_analysis_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** **Multivariate Environmental Similarity Surface (MESS)** identifies areas where models extrapolate beyond training conditions.
- **Green:** Interpolation zone (within training range) - reliable predictions
- **Red:** Extrapolation zone (novel climates) - higher uncertainty, interpret with caution

# Future Projections

## Future Habitat Suitability - Global (Clamped)
```{r future-global-clamped, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("future_clamped_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Predicted habitat suitability under future climate scenario **SSP585 (2021-2040)**. Clamped predictions provide conservative estimates suitable for management decisions. SSP585 represents high greenhouse gas emissions pathway.

---

## Future Habitat Suitability - Europe Focus (Clamped)
```{r future-europe-clamped, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("future_europe_clamped_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Regional view of future suitability across Europe. Compare with current Europe map to identify areas of range expansion, contraction, or stability.

---

## Future Distribution - Unclamped Predictions
```{r future-global-unclamped, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("future_unclamped_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Future predictions without clamping. Differences from clamped version indicate where future climates are outside the training range, requiring model extrapolation.

# Climate Change Impacts

## Climate Change Impact - Suitability Difference
```{r climate-change, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("climate_change_impact_clamped_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Change in habitat suitability (Future - Current).
- **Green:** Suitability increases (range expansion potential)
- **Yellow:** Little change (stable habitat)
- **Red:** Suitability decreases (range contraction risk)

Subtitle shows mean change and % area with gains vs. losses.

---

## Binary Predictions - Current Suitable Habitat
```{r binary-current, out.width='70%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("th_current_binary_maxTSS_clamped.png")))
```

**Description:** Binary presence/absence map using **maximum TSS threshold**. Shows areas predicted as suitable (presence) vs. unsuitable (absence) under current conditions. Useful for conservation planning and identifying core habitat.

---

## Binary Predictions - Future Suitable Habitat
```{r binary-future, out.width='70%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("th_future_binary_maxTSS_clamped.png")))
```

**Description:** Binary map for future climate. Compare with current binary map to identify:
- Areas of **habitat loss** (present now, absent future)
- Areas of **habitat gain** (absent now, present future)
- **Stable habitat** (present in both periods)

# Clamping Analysis

## Clamping Effect - Current Climate
```{r clamp-effect-current, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("clamp_effect_current_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Difference between unclamped and clamped predictions for current climate (Unclamped - Clamped).
- **Blue:** Unclamped predicts lower suitability
- **White:** No clamping effect (within training range)
- **Red:** Unclamped predicts higher suitability

Low values indicate most current climate is within training data range.

---

## Clamping Effect - Future Climate
```{r clamp-effect-future, out.width='85%'}
include_graphics(file.path(species_folder, paste0("clamp_effect_future_", species_key, ".png")))
```

**Description:** Clamping effect for future climate. Larger differences (more red/blue) indicate novel climate conditions requiring extrapolation. Higher % affected suggests greater uncertainty in future projections.

---

# Ranking 

| Species | Current Mean | Future Mean | Threat Change | Change (%) | Threat Category |
|:--------|-------------:|------------:|--------------:|-----------:|:----------------|
| *Harpegnathos saltator* | 0.126 | 0.127 | 0.001 | 0.8% | Moderate |
| *Camponotus pilicornis* | 0.060 | 0.066 | 0.006 | 9.9% | Low |
| *Leptomyrmex erythrocephalus* | 0.040 | 0.057 | 0.016 | 40.8% | Low |
| *Camponotus cruentatus* | 0.037 | 0.048 | 0.012 | 32.6% | Low |
| *Monomorium carbonarium* | 0.036 | 0.067 | 0.031 | 87.7% | Low |
| *Camponotus foreli* | 0.006 | 0.007 | 0.001 | 18.2% | Minimal |
| *Monomorium subopacum* | 0.006 | 0.007 | 0.002 | 29.0% | Minimal |
| *Camponotus lateralis* | 0.005 | 0.011 | 0.006 | 107% | Minimal |
| *Camponotus fallax* | 0.004 | 0.015 | 0.011 | 307% | Minimal |
| *Camponotus aethiops* | 0.003 | 0.007 | 0.004 | 110% | Minimal |


# Summary & Conclusions

## Key Findings

**Model Performance:**
- High discrimination ability (see ROC curve and AUC values)
- Cross-validated predictions show good consistency (low uncertainty)
- Selected variables show clear ecological relationships

**Current Distribution:**
- Predicted suitable habitat matches known distribution patterns
- Core suitable areas identified
- Low extrapolation in current climate (MESS analysis)

**Future Projections (SSP585 2021-2040):**
- Climate change impacts on suitability distribution
- Areas of potential range expansion and contraction identified
- Novel climate conditions in some regions require cautious interpretation

---

## Model Limitations & Considerations

**Clamping:**
- Used clamped predictions for conservative estimates
- Future projections show moderate extrapolation in some areas
- Unclamped predictions available for sensitivity analysis

**Assumptions:**
- Species is in equilibrium with climate
- No dispersal limitations
- No biotic interactions considered
- No adaptation or evolution

**Uncertainty:**
- Higher uncertainty in extrapolation zones (MESS analysis)
- Model represents climatic niche, not realized distribution

---

## Conservation & Management Implications

**Priority areas for conservation:**
- Core suitable habitat (high suitability, low uncertainty)
- Stable habitat under future scenarios
- Connectivity corridors between current and future suitable areas

**Climate change adaptation:**
- Identify refugia (areas maintaining suitability)
- Plan for assisted migration if needed
- Monitor populations in areas of predicted decline

**Further research:**
- Field validation of predictions
- Incorporate dispersal modeling
- Model other emission scenarios (SSP245, SSP370)
- Ensemble modeling with multiple algorithms

---

## Data & Methods Summary

**Occurrence data sources:**
- GBIF, iNaturalist, BISON, ALA, iDigBio
- Quality controlled and spatially thinned

**Environmental variables:**
- Bioclimatic variables (WorldClim)
- Land cover data
- Selected via correlation analysis

**Modeling approach:**
- MaxEnt algorithm (Maxnet implementation)
- Hyperparameter optimization via genetic algorithm
- 5-fold cross-validation
- Clamped predictions for final maps

**Future scenario:**
- SSP585 (high emissions)
- Time period: 2021-2040

---

## References

**Key Papers:**

- Elith, J., et al. (2011). A statistical explanation of MaxEnt for ecologists. *Diversity and Distributions*, 17(1), 43-57.

- Merow, C., et al. (2013). A practical guide to MaxEnt for modeling species' distributions. *Ecography*, 36(10), 1058-1069.

- Vignali, S., et al. (2020). SDMtune: An R package to tune and evaluate species distribution models. *Ecology and Evolution*, 10(20), 11488-11506.

- Phillips, S.J., et al. (2017). Opening the black box: an open-source release of Maxent. *Ecography*, 40(7), 887-893.

**Data sources:**
- WorldClim 2.1 bioclimatic variables
- GBIF occurrence data
- CMIP6 future climate projections

# Thank You

## Contact & Additional Information

**For more information:**
- See technical documentation in species folder
- Tables with detailed results available in `tables/` subfolder
- High-resolution rasters available in `rasters/` subfolder

**Output files include:**
- Suitability predictions (current, future, change)
- Binary presence/absence maps
- Response curves for all variables
- Uncertainty and MESS analyses
- Model evaluation metrics
- Variable importance tables

**Questions?**

---

## Appendix: Output File Structure
```
species/1314580_Monomorium_minimum_(Buckley,_1867)/SDM_maxnet/
├── rasters/
│   ├── current_clamped_1314580.tif
│   ├── future_clamped_1314580.tif
│   ├── change_clamped_1314580.tif
│   ├── uncertainty_1314580.tif
│   └── mess_1314580.tif
├── tables/
│   ├── summary_1314580.xlsx
│   ├── final_variable_importance_1314580.xlsx
│   ├── jackknife_results_1314580.xlsx
│   └── optimization_results_1314580.xlsx
├── response_curves/
│   └── [individual response curve plots]
└── [visualization PNG files]
```
